description: NVDA Investment Overview segmentation pilot is complete. Three source-mapped eligible L2 tasks have been added; B01 is not strictly supported under ordinary numeric tolerance and is marked supported_with_presentation_caveat under a display-precision/truncation rule.

blocked_or_review_candidates.reason: Forward consensus revenue estimates require dated consensus or model-source basis.
CAGR endpoint is ambiguous; requires period clarification before formal task entry.
EBITDA is not directly reported in mapped primary source; requires reproducible definition and inputs.
EBITDA margin depends on EBITDA definition; downgraded to H1 pending reproducible basis.
Causal attribution claim is narrative inference, not deterministic numeric task.
Peer comparison requires defensibility review and peer-source mapping.
EV/EBITDA multiple lacks dated market-value and EBITDA basis.
Forward PE requires dated price and EPS estimate basis.
Forward-looking investment conclusion requires H1 human review.

schema.claim_scope: fact | calculation | valuation | comparison | inference | risk_disclosure

tasks.report_excerpt: Nvidia has delivered a remarkable financial performance over the past three years, with revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024, representing a 125.9% year-over-year increase.
tasks.claim_quote: revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024
tasks.normalized_claim: NVDA revenue was approximately $26.9B in FY2023 and $60.9B in FY2024.
tasks.extraction_note: Split from the same sentence; this task verifies the two revenue values, not the growth calculation.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

tasks.evidence_excerpt: Revenue table reports total revenue of $60,922 million for Jan 28, 2024 and $26,974 million for Jan 29, 2023.
tasks.review_note: Supported with presentation caveat. B01 remains eligible L2 but is excluded from strict supported count.
tasks.presentation_caveat: FY2023 source value is $26.974B and report value is $26.9B. This is truncation-compatible at one decimal place, not ordinary rounding, and exceeds NUM_ABS_001 relative tolerance.

tasks.report_excerpt: Nvidia has delivered a remarkable financial performance over the past three years, with revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024, representing a 125.9% year-over-year increase.
tasks.normalized_claim: NVDA FY2024 revenue growth from FY2023 revenue was 125.9%.
tasks.extraction_note: Derived calculation separated from the two reported revenue values.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

tasks.evidence_excerpt: Revenue table reports $60,922 million for FY2024 and $26,974 million for FY2023, with total revenue change of 126%.
tasks.review_note: Supported using exact source values; report's rounded revenue display would not independently reproduce 125.9%.

tasks.report_excerpt: Operational leverage is evident, with SG&A as a percentage of revenue declining from 9% in FY2023 to just 2.1% in FY2026, supporting substantial contribution margin improvement.
tasks.claim_quote: SG&A as a percentage of revenue declining from 9% in FY2023 to just 2.1% in FY2026
tasks.normalized_claim: NVDA SG&A margin was approximately 9% in FY2023 and 2.1% in FY2026.
tasks.extraction_note: Numeric SG&A margin claim separated from 'operational leverage is evident' inference.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 25, 2026

tasks.evidence_excerpt: FY2026 10-K consolidated statement reports revenue of $215,938 million and sales, general and administrative expense of $4,579 million for Jan 25, 2026.
tasks.review_note: Supported within predefined 0.1 percentage-point tolerance for numeric SG&A margin only; operational leverage inference is not scored.
